heterologous_genes.function: L-lactate dehydrogenase; converts pyruvate + NADH + H+ to L-lactate + NAD+
heterologous_genes.activity_regulation: Allosterically activated by fructose 1,6-bisphosphate (FBP) under acidic conditions; requires FBP + Mn2+ under neutral conditions

known_mutations.effect: No catalytic activity without FBP; marked activity with 5 mM FBP
Lower thermoresistance; not thermostabilized by FBP or Mn2+

heterologous_genes.expression_notes: Soluble cytoplasmic protein, no TM or signal peptide. FBP allosteric activation is advantageous in yeast since FBP is a glycolytic intermediate present at high levels. GRAVY score -0.08 (hydrophilic), estimated pI 6.9. Multiple crystal structures available for rational engineering.

alternative_enzyme_candidates.notes: Fungal origin (eukaryotic), potentially better codon compatibility and protein folding in yeast. Stable predicted (instability index 35.0). No FBP allosteric regulation reported. Cytoplasmic, soluble, no TM or signal peptide.
Thermostable bacterial LDH. FBP-allosteric activation. 3 PDB structures. May have advantages in thermotolerance.
Alternative bacterial LDH from dairy lactic acid bacterium.

knockouts.rationale: Major pyruvate decarboxylase isozyme; blocks ethanol pathway by preventing pyruvate to acetaldehyde conversion. OR-logic GPR (YGR087C or YLR134W or YLR044C) requires all three PDC genes to be knocked out.
Second pyruvate decarboxylase isozyme; OR-logic GPR requires multi-gene knockout to fully disable PYRDC.
Third pyruvate decarboxylase isozyme; completes elimination of pyruvate decarboxylation.
Major alcohol dehydrogenase; blocks acetaldehyde to ethanol conversion. OR-logic GPR (YOL086C or YGL256W or YBR145W) requires all three ADH genes to be knocked out.
Alcohol dehydrogenase isozyme; OR-logic GPR requires multi-gene knockout.
Alcohol dehydrogenase isozyme; completes elimination of ethanol production.

Degrades L-lactate back to pyruvate via cytochrome c-dependent oxidation. Essential knockout to prevent product degradation. CYB2 interacts with DLD1/DLD2/DLD3, CDC19, NDE1/NDE2 in STRING DB (combined scores 0.94-0.99).
Blocks glycerol biosynthesis pathway, redirecting NADH toward LDH reaction for lactate production.

overexpressions.rationale: Increase pyruvate supply from PEP; ensures high substrate availability for heterologous LDH. PYK has OR-logic GPR (YAL038W or YOR347C).

native_transport.reaction: L_LACt2r (L-lactate reversible transport via proton symport)
native_transport.notes: Native carboxylic acid transporter. Model already includes L-lactate exchange reaction (EX_lac__L_e). JEN1 overexpression may further improve export at high titers.

optimization_rounds.strategy: KO PDC1/PDC5/PDC6 + ADH1/ADH3/ADH5 + CYB2 + GPD1, OE PYK1
optimization_rounds.notes: 17 reactions disabled. Near-theoretical maximum achieved. Stopped: <5% improvement over baseline.
Slightly higher product flux under microaerobic conditions.
No biomass produced under anaerobic conditions with all KOs; all carbon goes to lactate. Not viable for sustained production.

fba_results.strategy_description: Express heterologous L-LDH from L. casei (P00343). Knock out all three pyruvate decarboxylase genes (PDC1/PDC5/PDC6), three alcohol dehydrogenase genes (ADH1/ADH3/ADH5), CYB2 (lactate degradation), and GPD1 (glycerol pathway). Overexpress PYK1 to boost pyruvate supply. This eliminates ethanol and glycerol as competing pathways, prevents L-lactate degradation, and redirects nearly all glycolytic flux through LDH. With 10% minimum biomass constraint, achieves 19.65 mmol/gDW/h lactate production (98.2% of theoretical maximum 20.0). Production envelope shows approximately linear tradeoff: at full biomass (0.287) production is 15.83; at zero biomass production reaches 20.0.

L_casei_P00343.allosteric_activation: FBP (fructose 1,6-bisphosphate) under acidic conditions; FBP + Mn2+ under neutral conditions
L_casei_P00343.assessment: Excellent candidate: high Vmax, well-characterized kinetics across pH range, FBP activation beneficial since FBP is abundant in glycolysis, multiple crystal structures for engineering

R_oryzae_Q9P4B6.assessment: Fungal origin (eukaryotic) may improve expression in yeast. Stable protein (instability index 35). Less kinetic data available. No FBP regulation simplifies engineering but loses glycolytic coupling.

G_stearothermophilus_P00344.assessment: Thermostable. FBP-dependent. 3 PDB structures. Known R157Q mutation affects subunit assembly.

expression_risk_assessment.transmembrane_domains: None - soluble protein, low expression risk
expression_risk_assessment.subcellular_targeting: Cytoplasmic in source organism, should express cytoplasmically in yeast
expression_risk_assessment.cofactor_requirements: NAD+/NADH only - abundantly available in yeast cytoplasm
expression_risk_assessment.protein_folding: Homotetramer assembly required; bacterial proteins generally fold well in yeast cytoplasm
expression_risk_assessment.codon_optimization: Recommended for L. casei gene (GC content mismatch with S. cerevisiae)

cyb2_interaction_analysis.note: CYB2 knockout to prevent lactate degradation. STRING analysis confirms CYB2 interacts with DLD1 (score 0.998), DLD2 (0.978), DLD3 (0.976), CDC19/PYK (0.954). Functional enrichment confirms monocarboxylic acid metabolism (FDR=8.5e-8) and lactate metabolism (FDR=1.4e-5). Knocking out CYB2 disrupts L-lactate cytochrome c oxidoreductase without affecting essential pathways.

recommended_conditions.oxygen_level: Aerobic (default) or microaerobic (O2 lb=-0.5) for slightly higher yield
special_notes: L. casei LDH is allosterically activated by FBP, which is advantageous since FBP levels are high during active glycolysis in yeast
At low pH (4.8-5.5), LDH has lower Km for pyruvate (0.45-0.71 mM) and higher Vmax, favoring production at the naturally low pH of yeast fermentation
JEN1 (native lactate transporter) is present in the model; overexpression may be needed at high titers to avoid intracellular accumulation
PDC-null strains (delta-pdc1/pdc5/pdc6) are viable but grow slowly on glucose; adaptive laboratory evolution may be needed for robust growth

known_limitations: FBA provides flux values (mmol/gDW/h) not absolute titers (g/L); actual productivity depends on biomass density and fermentation conditions
Protein expression levels, folding efficiency, and in vivo solubility of heterologous LDH cannot be predicted by FBA
Product toxicity: L-lactic acid at high concentrations (>100 g/L) inhibits cell growth and may require adaptive evolution or acid tolerance engineering

PDC-null yeast strains (pdc1/pdc5/pdc6 triple knockout) require C2 supplements (ethanol or acetate) for growth on glucose due to loss of cytosolic acetyl-CoA supply; this is not captured in standard FBA
Allosteric regulation of L. casei LDH by FBP is not modeled in FBA but is beneficial in vivo
The model does not account for metabolite channeling, enzyme kinetics, or regulatory networks
Actual Km values at yeast cytoplasmic pH (~7.0) show reduced pyruvate affinity (12 mM); multi-copy LDH expression may be needed to compensate
